title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Easy Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) - Indian
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow Indian recipe. Perfect for a delicious lunch or dinner.
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken recipe step by step
seo.h1: The Best Homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Butter Chicken
Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, creamy flavors of our authentic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic!

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This popular recipe balances sweet and savory flavors with aromatic spices, making it a truly comforting and satisfying meal. Perfect for a hearty lunch or an elegant dinner, it pairs wonderfully with naan or rice.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken thoroughly. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes to the pan. Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the pan along with a pinch of salt. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to break down.

instructions.title: Blend and Finish the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture, pressing on the solids. Return the blended sauce to the pan over low heat.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the cooked chicken pieces, heavy cream, crushed fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), and sugar. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the sauce flavors and the sauce to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For an extra smooth sauce, ensure you blend the tomato-cashew mixture thoroughly and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This step is crucial for authentic Murgh Makhani texture.

Do not skip the kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves); it provides a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor essential to authentic Butter Chicken. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its fragrance.
Marinating the chicken for a longer period (up to 4 hours or even overnight) significantly enhances its tenderness and allows the spices to penetrate deeper, resulting in more flavorful chicken.
article:
Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is more than just a dish; it's a global phenomenon, a creamy, dreamy testament to the rich culinary heritage of India. Beloved by millions, this iconic Indian lunch recipe has transcended borders, becoming a staple in Indian restaurants and home kitchens worldwide. Its irresistible blend of tender chicken, simmered in a velvety tomato-based sauce enriched with butter and cream, offers a symphony of flavors that is both comforting and exquisitely indulgent. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore Indian cuisine, mastering this classic dish is a rewarding endeavor. Prepare to embark on a delicious journey as we delve into the secrets of creating authentic Murgh Makhani, a dish that promises to tantalize your taste buds and leave you craving more.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Murgh Makhani, literally translating to "chicken with butter," is an iconic Indian curry renowned for its rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato gravy. Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant, it was reportedly created by accident when chefs mixed leftover tandoori chicken in a tomato and butter gravy. The result was a culinary masterpiece that quickly gained popularity. Unlike many other Indian curries, Butter Chicken distinguishes itself with its characteristic sweetness, delicate tang, and a luxurious mouthfeel derived from generous amounts of butter and cream. The chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices, is typically grilled or pan-fried before being gently simmered in the makhani gravy, ensuring it remains incredibly tender and juicy. It's a perfect Indian lunch recipe, offering a hearty and satisfying meal that pairs wonderfully with various breads and rice dishes.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of fresh ingredients to achieve its signature depth of flavor and creamy texture.

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   **Chicken:** 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces. Thighs are recommended for juiciness.
*   **Plain Yogurt:** 1/2 cup, for tenderizing and flavor.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (for color without excessive heat), 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, salt to taste.

**For the Makhani Gravy:**
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 4 tablespoons, for richness.
*   **Oil:** 1 tablespoon (neutral oil like canola or vegetable).
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon ground coriander, 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin, 1/4 teaspoon cardamom powder.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed.
*   **Cashews:** 1/4 cup, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes (adds creaminess and thickness).
*   **Water or Chicken Broth:** 1/2 cup, as needed for blending.
*   **Heavy Cream:** 1/2 cup, for the luscious finish.
*   **Fenugreek Leaves (Kasoori Methi):** 1 tablespoon, crushed (essential for authentic flavor).
*   **Sugar:** 1-2 teaspoons, to balance acidity.
*   **Salt:** To taste.

**For Garnish:**
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped.
*   A drizzle of heavy cream.

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe Instructions

Follow these detailed steps to create a truly spectacular Butter Chicken:

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate and the chicken to tenderize.

**Step 2: Cook the Chicken**
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this stage as it will simmer further in the gravy. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Makhani Gravy Base**
In the same pan (or a clean pot), melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the Kashmiri red chili powder, ground coriander, ground cumin, and cardamom powder. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.

**Step 4: Simmer and Blend the Gravy**
Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree) and the soaked cashews. Add about 1/2 cup of water or chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the flavors to meld.
Carefully transfer the hot mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid.

**Step 5: Finish the Gravy**
Return the smooth gravy to the pot over medium-low heat. Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter until melted. Add the heavy cream, crushed kasoori methi (fenugreek leaves), and sugar. Stir well. Simmer the gravy for another 5-7 minutes, allowing it to thicken slightly and the flavors to deepen. Taste and adjust salt and sugar as needed.

**Step 6: Combine and Serve**
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken evenly. Let it simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the gravy and ensuring it's thoroughly heated through. Garnish with fresh cilantro and a final swirl of cream before serving.

## Pro Tips for Perfect Butter Chicken

Achieving the perfect Butter Chicken, a truly satisfying Indian lunch recipe, goes beyond just following the steps. Here are some pro tips to elevate your dish:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skip or shorten the marination time. The yogurt and spices not only infuse flavor but also tenderize the chicken, making it incredibly succulent.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes (San Marzano are excellent) or very ripe, flavorful fresh tomatoes for the best gravy base.
*   **Silky Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy until absolutely smooth is crucial. For an ultra-luxurious texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any fibrous bits from the onions or tomatoes.
*   **Kasoori Methi (Fenugreek Leaves):** This ingredient is non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Lightly toast them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** A touch of sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. Taste your gravy and adjust the sweetness and salt to your preference.
*   **Don't Overcook the Chicken:** Since the chicken will simmer in the gravy, initially cook it only until browned and mostly cooked through. This prevents it from becoming dry.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile and incredibly satisfying Indian lunch recipe that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. The richness of the curry is perfectly complemented by simple, absorbent sides.

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, chewy naan, especially garlic naan or butter naan, is the quintessential pairing. It's perfect for soaking up every last drop of the delectable gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Fluffy, aromatic Basmati rice or Jeera Rice (cumin rice) provides a neutral base that lets the flavors of the Butter Chicken shine.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a lighter option, whole wheat Indian flatbreads like roti or chapati are also excellent choices.
*   **Cucumber Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based side dish with cucumber and mint can provide a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Kachumber Salad:** A simple salad of diced cucumbers, tomatoes, and onions with a lemon dressing adds a fresh crunch.
*   **Mango Chutney:** A sweet and tangy mango chutney can offer another layer of flavor and balance.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs, preferences, or to experiment with new flavors.

*   **Healthier Options:** To reduce calories and fat, you can use less butter and cream. Substitute some of the heavy cream with Greek yogurt or a lighter milk alternative. You can also grill or bake the chicken instead of pan-frying it.
*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** For a delicious meat-free version, replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Paneer Makhani is a particularly popular and flavorful choice.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder to control the heat. For more spice, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or green chilies. For less, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Nut-Free Version:** If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute the cashews with sunflower seeds or skip them entirely and rely on a bit more cream or a spoonful of cornstarch slurry for thickness.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic restaurant-style smoky flavor, you can employ the "dhungar" method. Place a small, burning charcoal in a small bowl within the curry, drizzle a little ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes. Alternatively, a dash of smoked paprika can impart a subtle smokiness.
*   **Sweetness Adjustment:** The amount of sugar can be adjusted based on the sweetness of your tomatoes and your personal preference. Some prefer a slightly sweeter curry, while others prefer it tangier.

Butter Chicken is a dish that truly embodies the warmth and richness of Indian cuisine. Its creamy texture and balanced flavors make it a perennial favorite, perfect for a comforting Indian lunch or a special dinner. By following these steps and tips, you can recreate this culinary masterpiece in your own kitchen, impressing family and friends with an authentic and incredibly delicious Murgh Makhani. Enjoy the process, and savor every spoonful of this beloved classic!
